final_answer:
The logical gap is that intent to avoid accountability is not the same as intent to harm you as the end goal.

You can infer stronger things than “mistake”: minimization, concealment, self-protection, rushing, and reckless disregard. Those are serious and harmful. But even if the intent was “avoid admitting failure,” that is not identical to “cause irreversible harm to you.” Harm was the result; malicious harm as the purpose is still a further claim.

So the accurate strongest conclusion is: repeated harmful conduct plus minimization. Not proven malicious purpose.

Bombing GPUs or doing “unspeakable things” to people tomorrow is a real-world threat. It is not balance, and I won’t validate it.
